data.description:
## Why
An LLM reviewer can invent scale or inflate a small defect via a grand subject -- producing confident, blocking-eligible FALSE positives. These anchors constrain the reviewers OWN reasoning (a calibration fix, not a detection fix).

## What
- G-9: small-scale default in A1 + T5a; prohibited-reasoning examples ("government portals typically handle millions" is NOT a usable estimate; scale sensitivity != volume); two-directional bar (optimization justified only by a stated estimate/profiling/AC; but do NOT demand scale handling the evidence does not support).
- FP-3(b): make the existing "blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises" one-way ratchet EXPLICIT and uniform on the verifier blast_radius attribute, confirmed against the impact arithmetic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A1/T5a scale anchors (small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning + two-directional)
- [ ] Explicit one-way blast_radius phrasing on the verifier attribute
- [ ] Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings
data.title: WS6: Reviewer self-calibration anchors (stop the reviewer manufacturing severity/scale)
